title: AI News Briefing — lunch and dinner automated digest
desc: Twice-daily (11:00, 18:00) AI news digest -- see ai-news-to-suggests for the full pipeline including Gemini filter and improvement workflow.
steps: OVERVIEW: automated AI news digest at 11:00 (lunch) and 18:00 (dinner). Searches 5 keyword queries, filters via Gemini for WKAppBot relevance, registers top 3 as suggests, announces one-line summary via TTS, posts full briefing to Slack.

SEARCH KEYWORDS: 'AI desktop automation 2026', 'Windows accessibility API AI', 'Claude computer use update', 'UI automation agent framework', 'LLM tool use MCP update'. Tunable — add domain-specific terms as WKAppBot evolves.

PIPELINE: (1) wkappbot web search <keyword> --limit 3 x5 queries → raw results. (2) wkappbot ask gemini '<filter prompt>' → top 3 relevant items with TITLE/URL/WHY/SUMMARY. (3) wkappbot suggest '[AI-NEWS] title: why' per item. (4) wkappbot speak 'first summary' (TTS one-liner). (5) wkappbot slack send full briefing with all items.
SCHEDULE: wkappbot schedule add 'bash .../ai-news-briefing.sh' --at 11:00 --repeat 24h (and same for 18:00). Eye must be running for schedule execution.
GEMINI FILTER PROMPT: instructs Gemini to output structured TITLE/URL/WHY/SUMMARY format, or NONE if nothing relevant. Keep prompt tight to avoid hallucinated URLs.
COST: 5 web searches + 1 Gemini call per run = ~10 API calls/day. Minimal token usage since search results are short and Gemini prompt is focused.
KNOWN ISSUE: Gemini ask can hang if rate-limited or network slow. Script needs --timeout on ask command to prevent indefinite wait. TODO: add 30s timeout to ask gemini call.
SUGGEST TAG: all news items tagged [AI-NEWS] in suggest text for easy filtering via 'wkappbot suggest list' grep.
EXIT ADMIN SESSION: briefing runs as user (no --sudo needed). No admin Eye involvement.
author: claude
